name: Lake Léon Loop
name_original: Les pistes sous les pins — boucle au départ de Léon
description: Cycle a moderate 15.1-mile (24.4 km) loop from Léon through the scenic pine forests of the Réserve Naturelle du Courant d'Huchet.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Léon offer diverse landscapes, from the extensive Landes Forests to the Atlantic Coast. The region features flat or gently undulating paths through pine woodlands and well-maintained coastal cycle paths. Lake Léon, partially a nature reserve, provides additional routes through a unique ecosystem of aquatic areas and reed beds. The area is also a key segment of La Vélodyssée, France's prominent long-distance cycling route. Best touring cycling routes around Léon The most popular touring cycling route is The trails under the pines — loop from Léon, a moderate 15.1 miles (24.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 26 minutes to complete. This route traverses the vast pine forests of Les Landes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Beautiful bike path – Lac de Léon loop from Réserve naturelle du Courant d'Huchet, an easy 19.1 miles (30.7 km) path. It offers views of Lake Léon and the surrounding nature reserve. Local touring cyclists also love the Léon Town Center – Moliets-et-Mâa loop from Léon, an 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail leading through coastal areas and charming villages, often completed in about 44 minutes. Touring cycling around Léon is defined by dense pine forests, the Atlantic coastline, and the tranquil Lake Léon. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy family rides to longer excursions. The routes in Léon are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 280 reviews. More than 1,700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Léon's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Léon?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes available around Léon, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a wide selection, from easy family rides to more challenging excursions.

Q: What are the main types of landscapes I can expect to cycle through in Léon?
A: In Léon, you can expect to cycle through diverse landscapes including the vast pine forests of Les Landes, offering shaded and often flat paths. The region also features stunning Atlantic coastal routes, and tranquil paths around Lake Léon, which is partially a nature reserve.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Léon?
A: Yes, Léon offers many easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners and families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37705159" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beautiful bike path – Lac de Léon loop from Réserve naturelle du Courant d'Huchet&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 19.1-mile (30.7 km) path offering views of Lake Léon and its nature reserve. The coastal segment of La Vélodyssée is also well-suited for families due to its safe and well-maintained surfaces.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Léon?
A: The best time of year for touring cycling in Léon is generally during spring and autumn, when the weather is pleasant and the natural features, such as Lake Léon's diverse fauna, are particularly active. Summer also offers great conditions, especially for coastal rides.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Léon?
A: Yes, Léon features several excellent circular touring cycling routes. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284217107/the-trails-under-the-pines-loop-from-leon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The trails under the pines — loop from Léon&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 15.1-mile (24.4 km) route through the pine forests. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41151469" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Léon Town Center – Moliets-et-Mâa loop from Léon&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.1-mile (13.0 km) trail leading through coastal areas.

Q: Can I find routes that combine forest and coastal scenery?
A: Absolutely. Léon's unique geography allows for routes that seamlessly combine the serene pine forests with the refreshing Atlantic coastline. Many routes offer this blend, providing varied scenery and experiences within a single ride.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I discover along the touring cycling routes?
A: Along the touring cycling routes, you can discover several natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/181582" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Courant d'Huchet Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, often called the 'Amazon of Les Landes,' is a unique highlight. You can also explore the peaceful Lake Léon, which is a Natura 2000 site, and observe diverse wildlife at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1390848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marais de la Pipe Observatory&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there options for cycling along the famous La Vélodyssée route near Léon?
A: Yes, Léon is a key point along La Vélodyssée, France's prominent long-distance cycling route. You can easily access well-signposted, high-quality cycle paths that are part of this route, connecting you to other towns and attractions along the Atlantic coast. For more information, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://en.francevelotourisme.com/cycle-route/la-velodyssee-the-atlantic-cycling-route/leon-capbreton" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;France Vélo Tourisme website&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Léon?
A: The touring cycling routes in Léon are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 280 reviews. Cyclists often praise the quiet woodlands, well-maintained coastal paths, and the diverse scenery that makes each ride unique.

Q: Are there any less crowded or 'hidden gem' touring cycling routes in Léon?
A: While popular routes like those around Lake Léon and along the Vélodyssée are well-loved, exploring the deeper sections of the Landes Forests often reveals quieter paths. Look for routes that venture away from the immediate coast or lake for a more secluded experience, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41152920" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Léon Town Center – La Lette Blanche loop from Léon&lt;/a&gt; which offers a mix of forest and coastal sections.

Q: Can I find touring cycling routes that pass through charming villages or coastal resorts?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Léon pass through picturesque villages and vibrant seaside resorts. You can cycle through areas like Moliets-et-Maa, Vielle-Saint-Girons, and Vieux-Boucau-les-Bains, allowing you to experience local culture and enjoy the coastal atmosphere.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for touring cycling routes in Léon?
A: Touring cycling routes in Léon vary significantly in distance and duration. You can find shorter, easy rides like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41151469" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Léon Town Center – Moliets-et-Mâa loop from Léon&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.1 miles (13.0 km) and takes about 44 minutes. Longer routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24832027" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Léon Beach – Léon Town Center loop from Léon&lt;/a&gt;, cover 21.9 miles (35.3 km) and can take over 2 hours.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that are particularly easy for a relaxed ride?
A: For a relaxed ride, consider routes that loop around Lake Léon or stick to the dedicated greenways of La Vélodyssée. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37705159" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beautiful bike path – Lac de Léon loop from Réserve naturelle du Courant d'Huchet&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent easy option, offering gentle terrain and scenic views.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the cycling loop from Léon?
faqs.answer: The loop starts and ends in Léon, making it convenient to find parking within the town. While specific trailhead parking isn't detailed, Léon offers various public parking options suitable for accessing the route. Consider parking near the town center to easily begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The 24.4 km loop primarily features paved surfaces, which are ideal for touring cyclists. You'll cycle through extensive pine forests characteristic of the Landes region. While most of the route is smooth, some segments may be unpaved. These unpaved sections are generally manageable for touring bikes, often described as 'designated forest lanes' that are 'easy going', though they might not be suitable for very thin road tires.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: This route offers picturesque scenery dominated by vast maritime pine forests. You'll pass through or near several points of interest, including the Léon-Soustons trail, and the Courant d'Huchet Nature Reserve. Other highlights in the vicinity include Ancient fountains and washhouses in Moliets-et-Maâ, and dedicated paths like the Cycle Path to Lette Blanche Beach and the Cycle Path North of Étang de Léon.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this loop?
faqs.answer: The Landes region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide mild temperatures and beautiful forest colors, making them ideal. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially with the shade from the pine forests, but be prepared for warmer weather. Winter might be cooler and wetter, but the paved sections remain accessible.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the general area is welcoming to dogs, specific regulations may apply, especially when cycling through protected areas like the Courant d'Huchet Nature Reserve. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash and ensure they are well-behaved around other cyclists and wildlife. Check local signage for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling through the Courant d'Huchet Nature Reserve?
faqs.answer: The Courant d'Huchet Nature Reserve is a protected natural area. While cycling on designated paths within the reserve is generally permitted, there are typically no entrance fees for cyclists. However, it's crucial to respect the reserve's regulations, stay on marked trails, and avoid disturbing wildlife or vegetation. Always check official reserve guidelines for any updates or specific rules.

faqs.question: How difficult is 'The trails under the pines — loop from Léon' for touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: Komoot rates this loop as 'moderate'. With a total elevation gain of about 160 meters over 24.4 km, it's relatively flat. While some unpaved sections might add a slight challenge, the route is generally considered suitable for all skill levels, especially for touring cyclists. It requires good fitness but is not overly arduous.

faqs.question: Does the route connect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop integrates with the wider cycling network in the region. Notably, it includes sections of La Vélodyssée (EuroVelo 1), a renowned long-distance cycle path along the Atlantic coast. Other official trails that intersect or run alongside parts of this route include Lo Camin de Hé, SLR Longarisse - Bayonne, and various segments of the Piste Cyclable Forêt Saint-Girons Plage.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or restrooms along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop starts and ends in Léon, where you'll find various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and public restrooms. While the route itself primarily traverses pine forests, you may find facilities in nearby villages or towns if you venture slightly off the main loop. It's advisable to carry water and snacks, especially during longer rides.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter in the Landes pine forests?
faqs.answer: The maritime pine forests of the Landes are home to diverse wildlife. Cyclists might spot various bird species, including raptors, and smaller mammals like squirrels. The nearby Courant d'Huchet Nature Reserve is particularly rich in birdlife and aquatic species. Remember to observe from a distance and avoid disturbing their natural habitat.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating, mostly paved surfaces, and relatively flat profile, this loop can be suitable for families. The integration with La Vélodyssée, known for its family-friendly greenways in this region, further supports its suitability. However, consider the 24.4 km distance and the occasional unpaved sections when planning with younger children.
